name: Agent4Edu Uses Learner Profiles, Memory, and Action Modules for Response Generation
statement: Agent4Edu describes LLM-powered generative agents initialized from learner profiles and equipped with memory, reflection, and action modules that support exercise understanding, analysis, and response generation.
population_or_context: Personalized learning and computerized adaptive testing simulation.
time_horizon: arXiv page submitted 2025-01-17, revised 2026-05-28, retrieved on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: The staged extraction records 18,045 time-ordered response records from 500 Chinese high school students and reports ACC, F1-score, and ROUGE-3 learner-simulation metrics.
caveats: The arXiv HTML confirms architecture, dataset, model, and top-line evaluation details but not appendix-level prompt text in this staged pass.
The method simulates learner responses for educational systems rather than rubric-scored student answers alone.
evaluation_method: Assessment of consistency and discrepancies between generated agent responses and human learner responses.
reported_metrics: ACC, F1-score, and ROUGE-3 for learner simulation; Agent4Edu (GPT-3.5-turbo) is reported at 66.70 ACC, 79.84 F1-score, and 37.97 ROUGE-3.
limitations: Appendix prompt-level details need further review before more specific prompt claims.
Generated responses are tied to the simulator's learner profile and action-module design.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The staged finding paraphrases the profile, memory, reflection, action-module, dataset, model, and evaluation descriptions in the arXiv HTML method and experiment sections.
